name: Dimensional reduction at the Planck scale
summary: Multiple approaches to quantum gravity independently predict that the spectral dimension of spacetime (d_s, the dimension probed by a diffusion process) runs from d_s ~ 4 at large scales down to d_s ~ 2 near the Planck scale. Asymptotic safety (Reuter), causal dynamical triangulations (Ambjorn-Jurkiewicz-Loll 2005, arXiv:hep-th/0505113), Horava-Lifshitz gravity, and loop quantum gravity all show this behaviour. The convergence across radically different QG programs is striking — either deep, or coincidence.

characteristic_numbers.note: CDT numerical: at long distances spectral dimension matches classical 4D.
Asymptotic safety: d_s -> 2 exactly. CDT: 1.80 +/- 0.25. Horava-Lifshitz: 2 by construction at z=3 critical point. Robust prediction across approaches.

principles_stressed.note: Some forms of dimensional reduction (Horava-Lifshitz) explicitly break Lorentz at short distances, predicting modified dispersion relations testable in GRB photon arrival times. Other forms (asymptotic safety, CDT) preserve Lorentz on average but predict scale-dependent effective metric.

history: Ambjorn, Jurkiewicz, Loll (2005), 'Spectral dimension of the universe', Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 171301, arXiv:hep-th/0505113 — first numerical CDT result. Lauscher-Reuter (2005) confirmed in asymptotic safety. Horava (2009) by construction. Carlip (2017, arXiv:1705.05417) systematic review: 'Why dimensional reduction?'. The convergence is conjectured to be a real feature of any consistent QG.

references.citation: Ambjorn, Jurkiewicz, Loll, 'Spectral dimension of the universe', Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 171301 (2005)
Carlip, 'Dimension and dimensional reduction in quantum gravity', Class. Quantum Grav. 34, 193001 (2017)
